title: CHROMIUM PICOLINATE
description: Chromium picolinate is a chemical compound consisting of trivalent chromium and picolinic acid, designed to improve the bioavailability of this essential trace mineral. It functions by potentiating the action of insulin, thereby playing a critical role in the metabolism of carbohydrates, lipids, and proteins.

research.title: A double-blind, placebo-controlled, exploratory trial of chromium picolinate in atypical depression: effect on carbohydrate craving
research.summary: This study found that chromium picolinate may significantly reduce carbohydrate cravings and improve symptoms in patients suffering from atypical depression.

research.title: Chromium Picolinate Intake and Risk of Type 2 Diabetes: an Evidence-Based Review by the United States Food and Drug Administration
research.summary: An FDA review concluded there is limited and uncertain evidence that chromium picolinate may reduce the risk of insulin resistance and type 2 diabetes.

sentiment.summary: Users frequently report success in curbing sugar cravings and managing 'emotional eating,' though many question its significant impact on actual fat loss without diet changes.

effects.label: Sugar Cravings:
effects.description: Highly effective for many users in reducing the frequency and intensity of intense sugar binges.

effects.label: Weight Loss:
effects.description: Clinical evidence shows a modest benefit (approx. 1kg over placebo), which users feel is only effective when paired with exercise.

effects.label: Insulin Sensitivity:
effects.description: Most effective in individuals who already have compromised glucose metabolism or high-sugar diets.

dosage.label: Common Range:
dosage.description: Most supplements and studies utilize doses between 200 mcg and 1,000 mcg per day.

dosage.label: Craving Control:
dosage.description: Users often find 400-600 mcg to be the 'sweet spot' for managing appetite.

dosage.label: Tolerable Upper Limit:
dosage.description: The Institute of Medicine (IOM) previously noted a lack of toxicity but sets general intake guidelines around 25-35 mcg for basic nutrition.

sideEffects.label: Kidney Stress:
sideEffects.description: Some users express concern regarding potential renal toxicity with long-term, high-dose usage.

sideEffects.label: Skin Reactions:
sideEffects.description: While rare, some anecdotal reports mention potential interactions or sensitivities when paired with intensive skin treatments like Accutane.

sideEffects.label: Hypoglycemia:
sideEffects.description: Potential for lightheadedness if taken on an empty stomach by individuals with already low blood sugar.

availability.label: Over-the-Counter:
availability.description: Widely available as a standalone mineral supplement or as a key ingredient in 'fat burner' stacks.

availability.label: Legal Status:
availability.description: Unrestricted and sold globally as a common dietary supplement.
